name: clodex
description: Autonomous development loop: plan, ship, run codex adversarial-review, and iterate on findings until approved or threshold/iteration cap is reached. v0.3.2 adds `--from-pr [<num>]` — a first-class entry path for mid-development PR handoff. When plan/implement/ship were done outside clodex, `--from-pr` bootstraps `.clodex/state.json` from `gh pr view` metadata, validates branch + HEAD-SHA parity against the PR, then jumps directly to Phase 4 (review-fix loop). PR number is optional (auto-detects from current branch). Bootstraps with `bootstrapped_from_pr: true` so the Phase 5 report discloses the origin. Mutually exclusive with `--resume`, `--continue`, `--reset-review-iter`, `--skip-brainstorm`. Halts on existing state, branch mismatch, or HEAD-SHA mismatch with copy-pasteable recovery hints (Rule 4: never auto-checkout/auto-pull). v0.3.1 reverted v0.3.0's PowerShell-preferred pivot — Bash remains canonical for all codex-companion invocations. Single-retry broker-wedge recovery preserved. PowerShell retained only as the narrow `Stop-Process` carve-out for broker-kill. Paired with HARD_RULES.md v0.3.1 (unchanged — `--from-pr` writes only to `.clodex/`, already allowed under Rule 2's positive contract). v0.2.9 lifted --version/--help into commands/clodex.md. v0.2.8 added broker wedge auto-recovery. v0.2.7 added iter-NNN-stalled.md breadcrumbs + ~3-min stall detection. v0.2.5 externalized hard rules. v0.2.1 adapted to codex plugin v1.0.4's disable-model-invocation. v0.2 baseline: hard rules, --continue flag.
